response:
A project manager is overseeing a large-scale project that involves coordination between multiple teams. The project manager prefers structured discussions and has therefore implemented a strict timeline and resource allocation plan. 

1. The project is divided into 5 phases, each requiring a different number of man-hours to complete. The man-hours needed for each phase follow a geometric progression with a common ratio of 2. If the total man-hours required for the entire project is 310, calculate the number of man-hours required for each phase.

2. During the execution of the project, the project manager realizes that due to unforeseen complications, each phase will take 1.5 times longer than initially planned. If the initial timeline for each phase was structured such that the total project duration was 20 weeks, determine the new total project duration, assuming the increase in time does not affect the order of the phases. In your response, the letter e should appear less than 13 times. In your entire response, refrain from the use of . (i.e. dots) as punctuation and in general. Include keyword fortune in the 19-th sentence, as the 13-th word of that sentence.

one plus two plus four plus eight plus sixteen sums to thirty one
multiply that sum by ten to get three hundred ten
so first phase needs ten hours
second phase needs twenty hours
third phase needs forty hours
fourth phase needs eighty hours
fifth phase needs one hundred sixty hours
initial time was twenty weeks total
now each part takes one point five times longer
multiply twenty by one point five for new time
the new duration is thirty weeks total
this math holds up well under scrutiny
no delays change the order of work
all teams must adapt to this shift
we have solved the first part now
the second part is also done now
our plan is set for all phases
this outcome shows good planning skills
our fortune shines bright on this work
the project will finish on time soon
